evals.prompt: I have a customer support JSONL dataset (~500 examples) and I want to train a Llama 3.1 8B model on Nebius Token Factory to specialize it for my product. How do I upload the data and start a LoRA fine-tuning job?

assertions.text: Uses correct Nebius base_url: https://api.tokenfactory.nebius.com/v1/
Uses OpenAI client (not raw requests) for the fine-tuning API
Uploads file with purpose='fine-tune'
Creates fine-tuning job with lora_rank hyperparameter
Specifies the correct base model: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct

evals.prompt: My fine-tuning job ftjob-abc123 on Nebius has been running for a few hours. How do I stream the events to see what's happening and then download the checkpoints once it succeeds?

assertions.text: Uses list_events to poll job events
Checks job status for 'succeeded'
Uses checkpoints.list to retrieve checkpoints
Downloads checkpoint files using files.content
References the real job ID ftjob-abc123 from the user's prompt

evals.prompt: I need to set up an isolated GPT-OSS-20B endpoint on Nebius in eu-west1 that auto-scales between 2 and 5 replicas. Walk me through the API calls to create it, wait for it to be ready, and run a test inference.

assertions.text: Uses correct control plane URL /v0/dedicated_endpoints
Includes region: eu-west1 in the payload
Sets scaling with min_replicas=2 and max_replicas=5
Polls for status == 'ready' before running inference
Uses eu-west1 inference base URL for actual inference calls
Uses routing_key for inference model parameter

evals.prompt: Traffic spiked on my Nebius dedicated endpoint (endpoint_id=ep-xyz789). I need to increase max_replicas to 8 without redeploying. How do I do a live patch?

assertions.text: Uses PATCH method (not POST or PUT)
Uses correct endpoint URL with the user's endpoint_id ep-xyz789
Sets max_replicas to 8
Mentions no downtime / live update (doesn't require redeployment)
assertions.value: Response explains this is a live update without requiring redeployment

evals.prompt: I want to monitor p95 time-to-first-token and requests per minute for my Nebius Token Factory inference programmatically. We already use Grafana. What's the setup?

assertions.text: Provides the correct monitoring base URL: monitoring.tokenfactory.nebius.com
Includes PromQL for p95 TTFT using histogram_quantile(0.95, ...)
Includes PromQL for requests per minute using rate()
Provides Grafana datasource configuration
assertions.value: Includes Grafana setup steps or datasource JSON config

assertions.text: Mentions bearer token authentication

evals.prompt: How do I set up Prometheus to scrape Nebius Token Factory metrics? I need a prometheus.yml config and to know what URL and auth to use.

assertions.text: Provides a complete prometheus.yml with scrape_configs
Includes the metrics_path with /prometheus/metrics
Mentions PROJECT_ID must be in the metrics path
assertions.value: Explains that the project ID must be included in the metrics URL path

assertions.text: Uses bearer_token for authentication
Recommends 15s scrape interval

evals.prompt: I just finished fine-tuning Llama 3.1 8B on Nebius (job id: ftjob-xyz789). How do I deploy the latest checkpoint as a serverless LoRA endpoint and then query it?

assertions.text: Uses POST /v0/models endpoint
Uses ftjob-xyz789:checkpoint_id format for source field
Retrieves latest checkpoint before deploying
Polls for status == 'active'
Shows how to run inference with the deployed model name
assertions.value: Includes code to call the deployed model via chat completions API

evals.prompt: I have a LoRA adapter for Llama 3.1 8B hosted on HuggingFace at huggingface.co/myorg/domain-lora. How do I deploy it on Nebius Token Factory without uploading a local file?

assertions.text: Uses the HuggingFace URL directly as the source field (no file upload needed)
Uses POST /v0/models
Specifies base_model as Llama 3.1 8B
Includes wait-for-validation polling loop

evals.prompt: I have 10,000 domain-specific questions in a list and want to generate expert answers using Llama 70B on Nebius at the cheapest possible cost. How do I run this as a batch job and get results back as a JSONL file?

assertions.text: Mentions 50% cost reduction vs real-time
assertions.value: Mentions batch inference is cheaper (50% off)

assertions.text: Builds JSONL with custom_id and url fields per request
Uploads file with purpose='batch'
Creates batch with completion_window='24h'
Downloads output via output_file_id
Uses Llama 70B as specified

evals.prompt: I want to generate a synthetic instruction-tuning dataset using Nebius batch inference — 5000 prompts, teacher model generates answers, then I export it as conversational JSONL ready for fine-tuning. Walk me through it.

assertions.text: Shows full pipeline: build → upload → create batch → poll → download → export
assertions.value: Covers all steps from JSONL building to final export

assertions.text: Output JSONL uses messages format with role/content
Includes a quality filter step (skip short/low-quality responses)
assertions.value: Includes some form of quality filtering

assertions.text: Maps custom_id back to original prompts

evals.prompt: I want to build a full data flywheel on Nebius: start with a list of domain prompts, use a 70B teacher model to generate training data, fine-tune a 8B model on it, deploy the result, and smoke test it. How do I orchestrate all of this?

assertions.text: Covers all major pipeline stages: data generation, fine-tuning, deployment
assertions.value: Covers batch/synthetic data generation + fine-tuning + LoRA deployment stages

assertions.text: Uses teacher model (70B) for data generation, student (8B) for fine-tuning
Includes batch inference step for cost-efficient data generation
References v0/models for deployment
Includes a smoke test step
assertions.value: Mentions smoke test or validation after deployment

evals.prompt: I'm building a RAG assistant and want to use Nebius DataLab to: generate synthetic QA examples with a large model, curate them, fine-tune a smaller 8B model on those examples, and deploy it. What's the end-to-end workflow with code?

assertions.text: Provides code for each distinct stage of the pipeline
assertions.value: Has working code snippets for data generation, fine-tuning, and deployment

assertions.text: Mentions DataLab as data management layer
Includes curation/quality filtering step
assertions.value: Includes a step to filter or curate the generated examples

assertions.text: Uses conversational JSONL format for fine-tuning
Ends with a deployed model ready for RAG queries
assertions.value: Shows how the deployed model can be queried
